reviews.review_description: This game is Amazing, for both quest 2 and quest 3. For the quest 2 users out there (like me) If you want multiple rooms without the headset complaining about to much walls. Just make multiple spaces marking every room of the house and just make sure to mark Tyne doors. Wonderful purchase for 11 Australian dollars
This pushed me to my absolute fear 😨 I thought fnaf help wanted was the scariest vr game  but this literally turned my home into a ghost realm

Finally something that shows what mixed reality can offer. Ignore the negative reviews, they are from before the developer included the auto-room-scan ability. Now it is just "Put on the headset and play". Developer is still fully involved and improving the game, very supportive. HIGHLY recomended.

Whenever I play a horror game it turns out to be a trash game. But when I played this game my perspective of horror games on vr changed. The scariest game i played at the time before hauntify was crawlspace VR. But now crawlspace has nothing on hauntify.
Thanks, i waited years yo finally find some good game that uses mixed reality

Is there going to be a haunted house environment overlay over your actual house on the next update on  what house look like a haunted mansion spooky lights on the wall goes is walking down your hallways that would be cool terrifying monster noises and monsters creatures

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for your interest in Hauntify and Mission Rift. I really appreciate the support!

At the moment, I am targeting a release toward the end of February 2026. I am still polishing, testing , and I also need to pass the Meta publishing review. I am hoping it gets approved the first time around so I can launch on time.

For the latest updates and questions, please join the Discord. I will share the exact date as soon as it is confirmed. Thanks again for the excitement and support. I cannot wait for you to play! https://discord.gg/dnFRvrjEJ9

reviews.review_description: I would recommend to horror fan really sacry

Its good

reviews.review_title: MR EXPERIENCE THAT IMPRESSES AND DISTURB
reviews.review_description:
MR HORROR IN YOUR LIVING ROOM
Hauntify Mixed Reality pushes mixed reality in a bold direction: here, your own living space becomes the arena for a horror game.
Spirits hunt you, exploit your furniture, appear where you least expect them—the simple act of turning your head is enough to trigger a jump.
The pass-through system is well-executed: the blend of the real environment and the virtual invasion is incredibly disturbing.
I was surprised by the effectiveness of the fear: your heart races, you turn around, you expect anything. It's an experience that prioritizes atmosphere over quantity.
WALK, FLEE, EXPLORE YOUR SPACE
The game remains a bit experimental, and some mechanics lack polish.
Calibration and space management can be frustrating for beginners.
But a video tutorial (on their YouTube channel) guides you through it, and it's actually quite simple to set up the game in multi-room mode. If you need additional help with setup or have any feedback, their Discord community is very responsive!
If you're looking for a VR experience that's off the beaten track and plays with your real-life environment and your senses, Hauntify is an excellent choice.
In short, an immersive, disturbing, and highly effective game! Every jolt is amplified by the closeness to reality: you play, but you doubt everything around you.
A title to recommend for fans of immersive horror—but approach with caution if you just want to play quietly.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this incredible review and for taking the time to describe your experience in detail!

Hauntify Mixed Reality is exactly about what you described turning your own space into something unsettling and making you second-guess every corner of your home, so it means a lot to hear that the atmosphere and fear really landed for you.

You’re absolutely right that some aspects are still experimental, especially calibration and multi-room setup. I’m actively working on smoothing those steps and adding more polish to the mechanics while keeping that “disturbing in your own living room” feeling strong.

Thank you as well for mentioning the tutorial and Discord, anyone reading this: if you ever get stuck or have ideas, I’m always around and happy to help.

Really appreciate the recommendation and your thoughtful feedback.

reviews.review_description: The developer was amazing! He even made me specific instructional videos to help me along. Very kind and highly recommend their apps, because in this day and age, it’s usually impossible to get a real person, and this developer went out of their way multiple times for me. I was very grateful to get a chance to retry the app for someone who so obviously loves what they do, and puts care into it. Thank you to the developer for all their time and effort.

developer_response.body:
Oh no :( Could you let me know which setup option you used—Custom Real-Time or Meta Setup? Having the correct setup for your play area’s shape and size is essential to things working as intended or else the game doesn’t work.

I’m a solo developer and still actively improving the instructions so players don’t get stuck. I’ve also reached out to you through the Quest 3 app if you’re open to it, I’d love to help you get a proper run and use your experience to make the game better to avoid other players experiencing the same thing.  If you refunded it I can give you a key to try again as I want to make it worth it for you

reviews.review_description: so  I was trying to find a good horror game and this amazing also really terrifying but fun, but it takestime to set up for me. It’s worth it. ￼

reviews.review_title: After 3 hours still cant figure it out..
reviews.review_description: After 3 hours trying to figure out how to get my room scans in the game, I gave up. And the hold A and scan room in the game doesn't work... refund please.

developer_response.body: Oh no :(.  I just reached out to you in the meta horizon app to help as a really care about this game and wanted to make sure you are able to play the game as setup is essential for the experience. The experimental custom scene setup option works for the quest 3 or 3s only. Spatial permissions must be enabled on the device otherwise the scanning won’t work. The user must press a wait and then it will start scanning and not hold. If you have a quest 2 I suggest the meta setup option to play the game.  If you refunded the game I can give you a free key to try again.  I may have to update the tutorials in the game to avoid confusion. I can send you a custom video so you can get it working in 2 min.  Quest 2 does not have the Scanning ability.

reviews.review_description: I tried to get the mesh to scan the room but it never would scan. I’m changing this to a 3 simply because the developer reached out very quickly to resolve the issue for me, I haven’t had a chance to get it working yet due to my lack of time to work on it but if I can get it working I’ll gladly change it accordingly.

developer_response.body:
Hey BornRebel, that is unfortunate  that you were not able to get to experience Hauntify. I reached out through the Meta app because this issue is almost always a quick setting adjustment in the Meta menu.

If you chose Meta Scan, you’ll need to set up your room outside the game in the Meta space setup/boundary/menu. If you’re using the Quest 3 Custom Experimental Scan, make sure to press A to start the scan as shown in the in-game instructions.

One-star reviews really hurt a small game like this, and I care about it a lot. I’m happy to help you get everything working via Discord or through the Meta app

I hope once it’s set up correctly you’ll consider updating your review so I know it’s worth continuing to add more content. If you refunded the app I can provide you access to the game again so you can experience the game how it is intended.

No difference so pls try to fix or else I wasted €7.00 but still scary and my brother said he pee pee his pants

This game has some potential. Set up takes a bit of time and still glitches a bit. I’ve tried to play several times but the cross seems to do absolutely nothing to slow or stop the ghosts. I have done the survival mode but don’t last long. Wish there was a way to make it last longer. As it is now it’s a one and done game. Nothing much to accomplish like unlocking equipment that might actually work. THE CROSS DOES NOTHING!

developer_response.body: Hi There, In order to use the cross the player must press A on the controllers to freeze the ghosts a few seconds.  For more content please check out story mode.  I am still adding content to the game as it is still early access.  I reached out to you on the Meta App incase you want me to add anything to the game for next update.  I really care  about this game and want it to meet player expectations.

reviews.review_title: The Best Mixed Reality Game For Quest!
reviews.review_description:
This really is the best mixed reality game I've played on the Quest. One that actually gets you moving through your home or play space and really let's the mixed reality tech in the Quest 3 shine when set up properly.

That does bring me to this game's only real weakness, the complex and lengthy set up, though I saw this is getting addressed in an update coming this summer so looking forward to that!

Keep up the awesome work!

I do not do reviews as it’s just not my thing but this game app deserves a review. I am a grown a** man and I can not play this without getting scared to the point of screaming like a child.  A loud man child.  I know what going to happen and every single time I scream. My wife laughs uncontrollably, the animals scatter  as they are not sure what the heck is going on.  I got this about a year ago but just used the scan feature and it’s now so fun.  Coolest use of mixed reality I have seen so far.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! Glad you enjoyed!

reviews.review_description: I like it, could need a little bit more polish and mor dynamic enemies. Hopefully the dev will continue!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!  I plan to continue development and add more content to the game.  I will add more polish in the future as the game develops.

reviews.review_description:
When i first loaded the game the ghosts would follow me around my house.
 (Specifically the lady with a knife) Literally walking after me.  After a few games they were just stuck in one place not moving at all.  They appeared to be walking but would not move.  I will uninstall and reinstall it again and try it out.  When the bugs are fixed this game will be insane!  Hopefully it all gets worked out.  

Update:  I rescanned my room and it definitely helped.  For the experience this game is awesome and worth every penny.  This is the kind of game you show your guests who have never experienced the quest.  I feel like it's a little buggy  but im sure it will be worked out.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, I’m the developer of Hauntify. I reached out to you on the Meta app messages to see how I can better support you and learn more about your experience.

When ghosts don’t chase the player, it’s usually because the floor wasn’t fully scanned. If there are holes in the mesh or the green nav mesh doesn’t connect during prescan, the ghosts can’t reach you. With Realtime Scan, the green navigation mesh isn’t visible since it generates during runtime, so if the floor isn’t fully scanned—or if the user doesn’t look down long enough—ghosts may stop moving.

For the best results, open spaces without too much clutter work best, and the Meta Scene Scan at the main menu is the most reliable option for ghost chasing. Realtime Scan is still experimental.

I’m continuing to update Hauntify, and feedback like yours helps me fix issues and improve the experience for everyone. Since every play area is different, I’d love to hear more about your setup and experience so I can make adjustments in the next update.

Thanks again for helping make Hauntify better!

reviews.review_description: I actually had wrote a kinda harsh review of this game, and my comments were actually replied by him, I’m redoing my review considering he takes his time out of his day did cares about his community by fixing shi that people want, he just notified me that he’s working on a update aswell, nice bro

developer_response.body: I am updating the game in a few days with story mode and experimental real time mapping, I really hope to try the game again once i update more content

reviews.review_description: I think you should Take some inspiration from Spirit Camera for the 3DS, its a game I've always wanted but never really got to play. This game kind of reminds me of that and I'm looking for something that scratches that same itch. Another note: Developer is actually a pretty chill dude.

It is scary! However, it does not seem to last long AT ALL. Hoping someone can cue me in on how it is supposed to work and work for a long bit vs just a quick game and die. I have to be doing something wrong.
HALP!!

developer_response.body: Hi there, This is the creator of Hauntify, I reached out to you on the Meta App message to help you out.  The setup is key and the size and shape of the play area plays a big factor.  For smaller play areas I suggest story mode,  either the block building game or the button press game work better for smaller areas. Try to run away from sounds or footsteps, some ghosts you can hear if they get closer.   Staying in on area increases the changes of ghost spawns.  I can help you directly through meta app messages.

reviews.review_description: I wish it had a mode where you can get jump scared infinite times without dying but a good game

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!  That is a good idea!

reviews.review_description: Really creative use of the boundaryless feature and procedural scene mesh on quest! Love the freedom it gives you! Super scary as well, good work :)

This is the most horrifying game for VR! It just got a new Q3MR update. Real-time mapping system is novel and great.
You can actually play it anywhere!

Great recommendations!
I've played every scary VR game and this is by far the best. Having said that, it's not always clear what the goal is or even how to play.
Every update make it better!

Great concept and very fun, very well executed fake flashlight in your own house. Only real problem is, it requires basically a full house.

If you're in an apartment, you'll just die in a doorway. If you have a house, it better be your house, and a decently sized one. When 2 bedroom doors are closed in your house because other people live there, it's a very difficult game. You'll basically end up running between the same 2 spots your broke butt can go to.

If you got a decent sized house, worth $7 definitely. If you're broke in a tiny dwelling, this ain't it.

Ignore the hate guys trust me this is one of those games that acutally give you adrenaline.

It would also be nice if you can add more animations like for example the granny or whatever that thing is can be making footsteps in another room, it might tempt you to check and you dont see her anywhere then when you point the flash up you see her crawling and the celing and she sprawls on you, i dont know if that made sense but hopefully you get the vision. Also id appreciate it if you can implement other modes with other than a cross for religious reasons as muslims play this too

reviews.review_title: How do I set it up with quest 2
reviews.review_description: How do you set it up on quest 2 it's so annoying these tutorials are not helpful

developer_response.body:
Hi There, Setting up walls for your scene setup occurs outside of the game:  To set up your Meta Quest space, you need to first complete the initial setup with the Meta Horizon mobile app and then perform a space setup within the headset by going to Settings > Environment setup > Space setup to scan your play area. There are tutorials on youtube https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qYlAELeqDoI

The game just uses what has been already setup through meta horizons space setup.
Some tutorials on youtube made by other users of meta setup process for quest 2  are outdated.

reviews.review_description: The people don’t even chase me for some reason but it looks fun I don’t know why I don’t work for me

developer_response.body: Hi there, this is the developer of Hauntify,  I reached out to you on the meta app messages to help and learn more how i can help.  When the ghost don't chase the player it is due to the floor not being scanned all the way.  The ghosts will stop if there are holes in the mesh and cant reach the player or the green nav mesh does not connect in the prescan option.  Realtime scan is experimental since it generates during run time (green navigation mesh is not visible) since there is not a good way to tell if something is scanned it can result in stopping behavior if not fully mapped or the user doesn't look at the floor long enough.  Open spaces without objects or clutter work the best.  I am updating the app again and the feedback helps me a lot to fix anything that pops up.  Meta Scene scan at the main menu is the most reliable option when it comes to ghosts chasing.  Developing this app is challenging since everyone's play area is different.  I would like to learn more from you so i can make any adjustments in my next update. I hope to learn more about your experience as it will help other players too!

reviews.review_title: So confusing and the game sucks
reviews.review_description: So confusing and the game sucks

developer_response.body:
Hey there, I’m the creator of Hauntify. I read your review and wanted to let you know I just updated the game yesterday. I’ve added a real-time scanning option as an alternative to Meta’s scene mapping, and introduced a story mode with deeper monster interactions.

I also reached out through the Meta app message in case you return to the game and want to give it another try. If there’s anything specific you’d like to see, I’d be happy to consider it for future updates.

Hauntify is still in early development and constantly evolving, your feedback and ideas are incredibly valuable to me. Reviews really matter to me,  and I want to make sure I’m doing everything I can to make it better for players.

Probably the best in this genre. Maybe add some spooky shadow figures lurking in dark wall corners and ghosts that float and pop up with creepy audio . That don't hurt you but will intensify the spookiness.

Just a thought.
Really nice game.
This is the bestest game that i have played on vr not only will it scare you but it will give you nightmares as well keep up the good work
I’m trying to make the walls in a different space but I can’t do it can I get help please

developer_response.body: Hi There, I can help through the discord server or through the Meta app (Spawnside @jo78970) I am working on a brand new custom setup system to avoid the challenges of using Metas room setup system.  I made a you tube tutorial to help, meta updates may have changed a few things, but I am happy to help.  I have been working hard on the new update.  Youtube tutorial video for room setup quest 3/3s.  Quest 2 is more of a manual process.  https://www.youtube.com/watch?app=desktop&v=_rBi9g4q0vI&ab_channel=VirtualGoGaming

reviews.review_description: For a game in development this is a lot of fun. Loving the feel of the game and looking forward to seeing where it will go next
I PISSED MY PANTS WHILE PLAYING THIS GAME AND I ALMOST HAD A HEART ATTACK
I had a blast running inside my house, cannot wait till more things are added and game updated, it has great potential. Keep it up!
Great game just not my style of play

It's definitely one of the first solid ideas out of the store. Even way back on quest 2, running around your house with different types of mods with different chase mechanics coming after you is just what you think this headset should be. It can be scary just trying not to get caught but it's also funny to see a random corpse randomly go from one room to the next. After understanding how to get it to work I definitely had a lot of fun.

Note: it's not perfect, I'm definitely someone who has had to put in a little effort to get the game to work. The guy behind the game has been very quick to help me personally to get to the bottom of it. 5 starts because the game itself is great and it's a good price and would love to see what keeps coming as the guy is still working on it.

developer_response.body: Hi there, sorry to hear! I will be able to help you get the game to work. I messaged you on the Meta App and am willing to take the time and provide you a custom video to aid you with the setup tailored on your questions.  I really care about this game as a solo developer and want you to enjoy the game.  The game now automatically disables the guardian. For the door cutouts if aligned restarting the game can fix this. Meta scene scan updates will address this issue. Developing mixed reality can be tricky since the experience largely depends the on kind of play area and Meta updates their scene scan setup often resulting in tutorial videos quickly becoming outdated. I please let me know if I can help as reviews really matter and motivate me to continue supporting the game.

reviews.review_title: Not sure what I'm even supposed to do
reviews.review_description: This game has no instructions beyond setup. No method of survival, and despite playing for less than two hours trying to figure out what the actual hell I'm doing, I can't refund it. The ghost models aren't bad but I don't even understand what I'm supposed to ACTUALLY do. I was expecting like. irl phasmophobia. I got "get chased down by a ghost with no escape and no goals" like I really wish this game had more to offer. Noted my play area isn't large, but it is multi room. I hope to see more and maybe in the next update focus on what can be done to make it enjoyable for those who have a small area to work with. Maybe a mode that isn't pass through so this game is enjoyable for everyone, a map that can be maneuvered on the joystick so your not SOL if your house isn't large.

developer_response.body: Hey there, I need your help to know what I can add to the game in the next update so I can make the experience worth it for you.  The goal of the game is to survive as long as possible and run away from the ghosts. The cross can be used to stop the ghosts to allow the player more time to escape but the charges are limited. Ghosts appear and disappear so they should not chase the player forever.  Play area shape and size are key to an enjoyable experience.  Small play areas make this game very hard to play in its current state, but I have a few ideas to make smaller areas more enjoyable.  Please keep in mind this is early access and I plan to add more to this game as a solo developer, but want to see if the idea/experience of hauntify is enjoyable before spending more resources on it.   I am saving up using the funds made from this game to hopeful someday hire a contractor to add more to the game.  Please let me know if you have any ideas I can quickly implement into the game as reviews really matter to me and i deeply care about this game as it develops over time. one star reviews can really leave an impact and hurt the game.  I reached out to you on the Meta Horizon app so I can learn from your experience to help other players and hopefully meet your expectations.  (SpawnSide @jo78970)

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed playing the game. I still can't get the multi room aspect to work though. Not sure how to cut out doors on quest 2. Still fun though.  Looking forward to more getting added. Some interactive objects like a Ouija board would be awesome! Maybe randomly make walls bleed. Great game though. I hope I can figure out multi room setup soon, but keep up the great work.

The game still needs some work, as there are a few small bugs, but the concept is really interesting. The developer also has a Discord channel to manage notifications about potential bugs, which I find very thoughtful. That alone makes it worthy of 5 stars, at least as a form of encouragement. I believe it will become one of my go-to apps to use when introducing someone to AR for the first time.

Great concept this game but needs some work on the ghosts as the ghost in the maid outfit is not very scary if it had such as shadow entities passes across doorways in the distance or appearing crouched down in corners it would provide much more in the way of scares 
But great job to the game creator

This is the best multiple room mixed reality game i have played so far on my quest 3. It's so immersive, it's genuinely scary!!. The directional sound does a great job of alerting you to danger and disorienting you. The flashlight mechanic is incredibly effective and makes you not want to run out of battery. Be prepared for some jump scares! If you are looking for a great example of how immersive a mixed reality game can be, this is it!
Is it multiplayer and if not plz make it bc me and my sis can play together

It's a super fun game even by yourself, but you can get bored, but of course, with friends, it's so much better, especially when you're casting it to your phone so they can watch you in game and in real life running around, it's overall one of my top 5 games and my friends can also agree, but always remember the risks of playing, me myself I haven't got hurt in anyway but it's possible so stay safe. Thank you.

For the price, any kind of jank is easily dismissible. It's a gaming experience you should definitely try out. It's also fun watching your friends run around the room begging for help.

It is black and white only on Quest 2, but honestly, it adds some tone.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review!

reviews.review_description: Can’t wait to see what the devs add to this game!

developer_response.body: Hey Ben, i am planning on adding more content to this base game. It is still early access and the experience depends on the size and shape of the play area. I am a solo dev, but I hope to use the funds made from this game to add additional game modes and content. I reached out to you through the meta horizon app to learn more on how I can improve. One star reviews are really damaging to the early access game and I really care about Hauntify and hope I can add more content to improve your review.

reviews.review_description: If your into horror this mixed reality is pretty good especially for early access, with multiple rooms setup I played in a 3 story house with zero issues. If your into mixed reality and horror this is a pretty cool game to try out
its early access and i would love to see its final product i’m giving it 5 stars so the creator can get the funds to complete this game

developer_response.body: Thank you very much! I am excited to add more to the game in the future!

reviews.review_description: Been playing scary games and watching scary movies my whole life. Never has a ghost waited for me to come out of my dining room to scare the living sh*t out of me though. 10/10

I understand this game is in development..but absolutely nothing worked and nothing was moderately enjoyable. It was so painful to even attempt to play.

The game simply is unplayable.

developer_response.body:
Hey Mike,

Just wanted to see if you had a chance to try the game again. I updated it with real time scanning
 
I really hope were able to experience full multiroom support for hauntify. 

I’d like to learn more about your experience so I can understand what went wrong and why you mention that it is unplayable for you. restarting the headset fixes any tracking issues and deleting existing room setup fixes any challenges, scanning the rooms using Meta's system can take a few attempts.  I’ve been updating the game frequently and truly care about making this app enjoyable for players. I reached out to you through the Meta app to get more insight, hoping to make sure the game meets your expectations. One-star reviews are something I’m working hard to avoid, and your feedback is essential to help me improve. 

Edit: After your review, I also had a few journalists test the game, and the setup worked well for them. It could be related to the headset or scan quality. Make sure you have the game updated to version 4.3. 
https://mixed-news.com/en/hauntify-multi-room-mixed-reality-hands-on/

I hope you’ll consider giving it another try. If you refunded it, I’d be happy to send you a key. 

Attached is a tutorial to help with the setup process.https://youtu.be/_INujp1AyV8?si=l8qWU_FBYCO7vc_x

reviews.review_description:
I would not suggest buying this game because the gameplay is really mid and the mixed reality is annoying.

it’s just sit there while ghosts teleport around you, not to mention that it takes a while to set up and costs money.

Even once you set it up the imperfections take you out of the game like the flashlight and entity interactions are really janky.

Overall wait for this game to be better, don’t buy now

developer_response.body:
Hey there, I’ve taken note of your feedback and I'm working on improving the experience. I’d like to hear more details about which parts of the setup or interactions with the ghosts and gameplay felt off to you. Your insights will help me refine the game further, especially with the mixed reality elements and overall immersion.  I reached out through the Meta app and could really use your help to improve Hauntify for other players 
Feel free to share more specifics, and I hope you'll check back once updates are released.

I updated the game and hope you can play it again

Best,

reviews.review_title: Doesn't work, quits after a second
reviews.review_description:
I'd really like to give more stars but I can't even try the game. After the update to v2.5 the first screen is shown and after a second the game quits on Quest 3 with v69.
Edit: I reinstalled the game and now it is getting worse. Not only that the game quits, my Quest restarts automatically after that or hangs up with passthrough picture freeze!
Edit 2: I could suddenly play the game but when the ghost caught me the game froze again. But the next try worked, I could play and finish a game properly.

developer_response.body: Hi there, this bug is really strange and I have not experienced this yet. Make sure to draw the boundaries to the max size and enable spatial persuasions before starting the game. It could be an issue with the headset . I would like to learn more about this issue.  I can reach out to you on the Meta App to learn more to see if there is anything I could do so this doesn’t happen to other players. Thank you for pointing this out.

I really care about this game, and the game was rebuilt again from the ground up. message dj(virtualgo llc) though the discord server https://discord.gg/53d4NQnDE9

To use the cross the player must hit X.  Please reach out to me if on Discord if you have any questions or need help, or ideas https://discord.gg/4RQ7M65X9x

reviews.review_title: I can’t set it up with my quest 2
reviews.review_description: This game is weird cause  I can’t set up the area to play in you should make a YouTube video about this game and show how to set up everything

developer_response.body:
Hey Faze,  I reached out to you on the Meta app and offered help.  I am solo developer and really care about this game.  There are Meta scene setup improvements since when you last played.  

Check out the following video to setup the quest 2 environment which applies to this game as well.  Please let me know if you have any questions on the Discord.  I am planning on updating the game to remove the boundaries so the players does not have to set up a dev account to disable them. In the mean time please watch the tutorial below. The new default manual setup only works for single rooms and my custom setup is only supported for quest 2 for now.
 one star reviews really hurt the games potential and I hope that you give it a second chance https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PnAmCeZo3oU&t=327s

reviews.review_title: Sadly it does not work on my quest 3
reviews.review_description: The game looks very very very fun and I give props to the developer/s on coming up with this idea but I spent my money on it and it wont let me play in my house. It only lets me do my living room and I spent like 1 hour trying to get it to work but it just doesn’t And my little brother was looking forward to playing it and I feel bad now that he can’t. But to the developer/s I really like this idea but since it didn’t work for me I have to give this only two stars and if I get it to work or the developer/s fix it then I will change my review or leave a better one that is five stars.

developer_response.body:
Hi Evan,
Thanks for giving Hauntify a try! The issue you’re describing is related to the Meta Quest 3’s spatial setup, not the game itself. To play throughout your house, each room needs to be scanned and linked in the headset’s Room Setup menu before launching the game.

Meta has an official guide on their website — search “Meta Quest multi-room setup” for step-by-step instructions. Once all rooms are scanned, Hauntify will automatically recognize and adapt to your full space.

reviews.review_title: Using quest 3 will NOT use spatial bound
reviews.review_description: Attempted 3 to 10 times to set spatial data and would only revert to stationary or manually room scale.

developer_response.body: Hey Matticus,  This appears to be un related to the game and rather the headset setup.  please update the headset and try again

reviews.review_description: I love this game its so scary and such a cool idea you should make it multiplayer like if your on the same wifi you can have multiple friends all running around inside a building or something this would be sick
No good for meta quest 3

developer_response.body: I made a new tutorial to help with make your experience better.  I reached out to you on the meta app if you need help.  Please give this game another chance after watching the new tutorial. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GhxQs_508Lc&t=34s&ab_channel=VirtualGoGaming

reviews.review_description: Really cool and this game could be even better with an update. Like Hiding spots?

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review, I am hoping to introduce hiding spots in the future. Summer has been crazy, I hope to find a good time to update the game.

reviews.review_description: make it so that if your connected  to the same wifi as someone, you could have their setup and if your in their house you can both play together

developer_response.body: Hi Danny, I am currently working on this as a separate project.  One day I hope to integrate multiplayer into my games.  Thank you for the review!

reviews.review_description: Definitely worth watching friends doing it

developer_response.body: Thank you! Glad you enjoyed Hauntify

reviews.review_description:
I was looking very forward to this but unfortunately the setup up process is as scary as it got.
I really wanted to enjoy this but gave up since dragging a menu around is tiresome and tends to make you tilt or delete walls all the time.
I'm sure it could be great but they really need to streamline the mapping section on start up. 
Have the selection menu pop in and out by the click of a button when not in use or something?
Sorry for the 1 star review but this is shocking considering its potential.

developer_response.body:
Hi OneCanDan,

I understand how frustrating setup issues can be. However, the wall setup system you mentioned is part of Meta’s platform and is separate from the game itself. The good news is that Meta has made significant updates to this system since 2022 to improve the user experience.

I’d love for you to give the game another try, as these updates may address the concerns you raised. Your input is valuable, and I hope the improved setup process allows you to fully enjoy the gameplay and potential of the app.

reviews.review_title: Nice and cool game but one suggestion
reviews.review_description: Once I saw this game, it seemed really cool cause I wanted an actual good mixed reality. Horror game. And it is pretty cool and fun but except one thing it’s really scary like we need something like power ups like a map or something so we can see where the monsters are. I think a map to see the entities would be helpful because they can sneak up behind you even if your back is on a wall.￼

This game continues to amaze me with the constant updates and new features being brought to the game. This is, i believe, one of if not the only, mixed reality horror games on the quest market. A must own for fans of the horror genre who want to experience it in their own home.
Fire game 10/10
This game is good but for some reason the board to play appears above me where i didn’t setup a room and I can’t play devs please fix this it’s a very fun game and experience. 4 stars

developer_response.body: Hi There, Thank you for the review!  I think resetting the floor height through the Meta main menu will correct the height of the in game menu.  I am available to help out on the Discord.

reviews.review_description: This game made me get scared way to much times 5/5 stars very recommended
This game is very fun but be careful! It can get super scary late at night

reviews.review_title: Great Concept and Very Scary Game!
reviews.review_description: Took some time to set up my home around 30 mins (tutorial on youtube helped).  Great to have multi floor and was very realistic.  Once you invest the time to set up you do not have in to do again.  Great scary game and had some friends try it as well.   Outstanding concept - keep developing games - very creative!!!

The game is very frightening, especially since the ghosts appear in your real home. Setting up the play space can be hard at times, but if you ignore the area glitching or moving around, it doesn’t really affect gameplay too much unless you are in an extremely large environment. Like a field with fences and small objects. I completely recommend this game to anyone who loves horror games.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I am glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: This game was released a long time ago and has gone through many updates. It used to be hard to set up, but with the new Quest 3 auto scan it is a lot easier. The game basically goes like this: You load in and your house goes dark. Eerie music plays while you walk around the house pointing your flashlight around for any signs of a ghost. When one pops up you run out of the room. While the ghosts/jumpscares could definitely be improved (they may have already been improved with the new update) this game has huge potential. Especially with the cheap price tag I would 100% recommend this game. Especially if you get scared easily or have never played a VR horror game before, this game will scare you. The Discord server for the game is also awesome you can chat with the developer he's super nice and will listen to any feedback for the game and try to improve it.
This game is scary as shxx. The developer support was first class. Anyone having trouble, don't despair and contact them. I have literally just stopped and have a cold sweat. Keep up the good work.

developer_response.body: Awesome! Thank you for the updated review!

reviews.review_description:
I play it on the quest 1 and it's very scary. It's the perfect game to play with friends and scare them!
Good job!

developer_response.body: Thank you! Glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: Bit more fiddly to set up than other games. The creator reached out to me and helped. Watch his YouTube videos before playing to understand how it works.
